nodes.context: African-American prison gang in the United States whose members are from Washington D.C.
Black prison gang allied with D.C. Blacks.
White supremacist prison gang, main rival of D.C. Blacks.
Large street gang outside prison system, mentioned for comparison.
Large street gang outside prison system, mentioned for comparison.

edges.evidence_quote: They are allied with the Black Guerrilla Family and some other black prison gangs.
edges.context: D.C. Blacks are allied with Black Guerrilla Family.

edges.evidence_quote: The Aryan Brotherhood is one of their main rivals.
edges.context: Aryan Brotherhood is a main rival of D.C. Blacks.

edges.evidence_quote: The race war in the federal system between the Aryan Brotherhood and the D.C. Blacks started on November 22, 1981, when a member of the Aryan Brotherhood, Thomas Silverstein, murdered a member of the D.C. Blacks, Robert M. Chappelle.
edges.context: Aryan Brotherhood and D.C. Blacks engaged in a race war with killings.
